title: CE Marking Evidence — Test Report Coverage (Art. 48)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'EU AI Act' → 'Art.13'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.13 (Transparency and information) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.19: CE Marking Evidence — Test Report Coverage (Art. 48).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.19' running on index=product sourcetype=plm:sku joined with index=testlab sourcetype=lab:report, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=plm sourcetype="plm:sku" earliest=-1d
| fields product_sku, model_family, hw_rev
| join type=left product_sku [
    search index=testlab sourcetype="lab:report" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(report_id) as test_report_id by product_sku
  ]
| eval covered=if(isnotnull(test_report_id),1,0)
| stats sum(covered) as ok, count as total
| eval coverage_pct=round(100*ok/total,2)
description: Joins product_sku to test_report_id coverage from test lab feeds for CE marking evidentiary chain.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes plm, product, testlab and sourcetypes lab:report, lab:report`, plm:sku. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Normalize SKU keys; (2) nightly DB Connect sync; (3) alert coverage_pct < 100 for EU SKUs; (4) map to notified body references outside Splunk. Indexes required: `index=plm`, `index=product`, `index=testlab`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=lab:report`, `sourcetype=lab:report``, `sourcetype=plm:sku`, `sourcetype=plm:sku``.
visualization: Single value (coverage_pct), Table (uncovered SKU), Pie chart (covered vs not)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for joins product_sku to test_report_id coverage from test lab feeds for ce marking evidentiary chain. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk ITSI (1841).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=product` `sourcetype=plm:sku` joined with `index=testlab` `sourcetype=lab:report`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize SKU keys; (2) nightly DB Connect sync; (3) alert coverage_pct < 100 for EU SKUs; (4) map to notified body references outside Splunk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=plm sourcetype="plm:sku" earliest=-1d
| fields product_sku, model_family, hw_rev
| join type=left product_sku [
    search index=testlab sourcetype="lab:report" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(report_id) as test_report_id by product_sku
  ]
| eval covered=if(isnotnull(test_report_id),1,0)
| stats sum(covered) as ok, count as total
| eval coverage_pct=round(100*ok/total,2)
```

Understanding this SPL

**CE Marking Evidence — Test Report Coverage (Art. 48) (EU AI Act)** — Joins product_sku to test_report_id coverage from test lab feeds for CE marking evidentiary chain.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=product` `sourcetype=plm:sku` joined with `index=testlab` `sourcetype=lab:report`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk ITSI (1841). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: plm; **sourcetype**: plm:sku. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=plm, sourcetype="plm:sku", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Keeps or drops fields with `fields` to shape columns and size.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **covered** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **coverage_pct** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: coverage_pct, covered, ok, test_report_id, total.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (coverage_pct), Table (uncovered SKU), Pie chart (covered vs not)
